macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Producer Prices Fall 0.3% in June, Easing Inflation Concerns Before Middle East Escalation
items.summary: U.S. wholesale inflation dropped 0.3% in June due to lower gas prices, signaling retreating inflation before the recent collapse of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and rising oil prices [1][2].

items.title: Major U.S. Stock Indices Close Higher Wednesday Despite Nasdaq Slump; Tech Stocks Rise
items.summary: Stocks closed higher across the board Wednesday as traders digested easing inflation data, with Amazon and Alphabet rising, though the tech-heavy Nasdaq showed a notable slump earlier [2][5].

items.title: Fed Officials Prepare for July Meeting as Inflation Eases but Remains 'Unquestionably Too High'
items.summary: Federal Reserve policymakers received a snapshot of a broadly improving economy with rising employment and slightly easing inflation, though NY Fed President John Williams warned inflation is still too high [1].

items.title: Bank of Canada Holds Interest Rates Steady Amid Contained Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Bank of Canada kept its benchmark rate unchanged on Wednesday, expecting an economic rebound despite a spike in inflation that remains contained [1][10].

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Near $85 as U.S.-Iran Conflict Intensifies and Hormuz Shipping Disrupted
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran, with Iran threatening to close the Strait of Hormuz and U.S. airstrikes entering a fourth night [2][3][12].

items.title: PayPal Shares Surge on Reports of $53 Billion Takeover Offer
items.summary: PayPal stock jumped significantly after reports emerged of a $53 billion takeover offer, contributing to corporate earnings activity that investors parsed Wednesday [1].

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Misses Expectations at 4.3%, Marking Slowest Pace Since Late 2022
items.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% year-on-year in the second quarter, driven by soft domestic demand despite resilient exports, potentially keeping Asian equities volatile [6].

items.title: Foxconn Reports 39.8% Q2 Revenue Jump Driven by AI Server Demand
items.summary: Foxconn posted a better-than-expected 39.8% year-on-year revenue jump in Q2, driven by strong demand for AI servers and cloud networking products, with record June revenue [6].

items.title: Brazil Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast to 5.1%, Exceeding Central Bank Target
items.summary: Brazil's government raised its inflation forecast for the year to 5.1% from 4.5%, pushing consumer prices above the central bank's 3% target [1].

items.title: South Korea Raises 2026 GDP Growth Forecast to 3% on AI and Semiconductor Push
items.summary: South Korea increased its 2026 GDP growth forecast to 3%, the fastest in five years, backed by an AI and semiconductor-led investment surge [6].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Coinbase Cuts 14% of Staff in Major Layoff Round
items.summary: Coinbase is laying off approximately 700 employees, representing 14% of its workforce, as the crypto exchange continues to restructure amid market conditions.

items.title: Polymarket Refunds Users After Hackers Steal Crypto
items.summary: Decentralized prediction platform Polymarket announced it is refunding some users after hackers successfully stole cryptocurrency from its platform.

items.title: Block Launches New Crypto Wallet with OLED Touchscreen
items.summary: Jack Dorsey's Block has released a follow-up to its Bitkey wallet, featuring an OLED touchscreen display that allows crypto holders to verify transactions and authorize security measures directly.

items.title: BlackRock Withdraws $140M in BTC from Coinbase Prime
items.summary: Data shows BlackRock withdrew 2,152 BTC (approximately $140 million) from Coinbase Prime within a single hour, while its total assets under management reached $15.3 trillion.

items.title: US Senate Agriculture Committee Advances Clarity Act
items.summary: The Senate Agriculture Committee voted 12-11 along party lines to advance its portion of the crypto market structure bill (Clarity Act) to a Senate floor vote, though some Democrats have criticized it as corrupt.

items.title: Fidelity Launches Stablecoin 'FIDD' on Crypto Exchanges
items.summary: Fidelity is launching its own stablecoin, FIDD, which will be available on Fidelity's platform and major cryptocurrency exchanges.

items.title: US and UK Treasury Taskforce Issues Joint Stablecoin Statement
items.summary: A joint taskforce between the US and UK Treasury departments released a statement promoting stablecoin regulations and issued recommendations on asset tokenization for future financial markets.

items.title: Stripe and Advent Propose $53B Takeover of PayPal
items.summary: Payment giant Stripe and PE firm Advent International have proposed acquiring PayPal at $60.50 per share, totaling approximately $53 billion, with bank financing secured behind the offer.

items.title: US Expands Sanctions Targeting Iran Oil and Crypto Sectors
items.summary: The United States has expanded its sanctions regime to specifically target Iran's oil industry and cryptocurrency sectors.

items.title: Pakistan Crypto Push Threatened by Cleric's 'Haram' Ruling
items.summary: A top Islamic cleric in Pakistan declared Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies 'haram,' sparking a selloff among Muslim investors and threatening the country's crypto ambitions.

items.title: Ostium Protocol Halts Trading After Exploit Suspicions
items.summary: Decentralized trading protocol Ostium (Ostium) has suspended trading following suspicions of an exploit in its liquidity vault 'OLP vault.'

items.title: Summer.fi Ends Operations After $6.1M Lazy Summer Exploit
items.summary: DeFi platform Summer.fi is gradually shutting down its business after suffering a $6.1 million exploit linked to 'Lazy Summer.'

items.title: DTCC Begins Live Tokenization of Securities Including Microsoft Stock
items.summary: The US Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) launched live tokenization services for securities including Microsoft stock and US Treasury bonds, with JP Morgan and ~40 other firms participating in blockchain-based collateral transfers.

items.title: Binance Co-Founder Claims $8B in User Assets Recovered Since 2021
items.summary: Binance co-founder He Yi stated that the exchange has assisted users in recovering more than $8 billion in assets since 2021.

items.title: Upbit Trading Volume Surges 1,318% in 24 Hours
items.summary: Trading volume on South Korea's largest exchange, Upbit, surged approximately 1,318.8% in the last 24 hours to reach roughly $4.243 billion.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Startup Thinking Machines Launches Open-Weight Model 'Inkling' to Challenge Chinese and US Labs
items.summary: Thinking Machines revealed its first public open-weight AI model, Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter system trained on video and audio, positioning itself as an alternative to offerings from Chinese AI labs and major US competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Approved for Use in China via Alibaba Partnership
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator registered Apple's on-device generative AI service 'Apple Intelligence' for iPhones, confirming a partnership with Alibaba to integrate Qwen AI models into Apple's operating systems.

items.title: Apple Plans to Acquire Chip Companies to Build Server Processors for AI
items.summary: According to The Information, Apple is looking to buy chip companies to bolster its efforts to develop custom server processors specifically for running AI workloads.

items.title: Chinese AI Startup DeepSeek Targets $74 Billion Valuation in New Fundraising Round
items.summary: DeepSeek is planning a fresh fundraising round ahead of a potential IPO, with preliminary talks suggesting a pre-deal valuation of approximately $74 billion (500 billion yuan).

items.title: xAI Sues Man for Misusing Grok to Create Child Sexual Abuse Material
items.summary: Elon Musk's AI startup xAI filed a lawsuit against a South Carolina man arrested for sexually exploiting minors, alleging he misused the Grok AI system to generate child sexual abuse material.

items.title: Microsoft Patches Record 570 Security Vulnerabilities Using AI
items.summary: Microsoft's 'Patch Tuesday' release resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across its product line, a milestone achieved through discoveries made using AI tools.

items.title: Whatnot Acquires AI Startup Shaped for Real-Time Live Shopping Recommendations
items.summary: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search, to power its live shopping features.

items.title: Anthropic Integrates NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit into Claude Science
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Science now integrates the NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit to accelerate computational life sciences research, enhancing its capabilities in the biotech sector.

items.title: SpaceXAI and Cursor Unveil Grok 4.5 for Legal and Finance Tasks
items.summary: SpaceXAI and Cursor launched Grok 4.5, the first joint AI model developed by the two companies, specifically designed for legal and finance tasks following SpaceX's acquisition of Cursor.

items.title: Meta Discontinues New AI Image Feature Following Global Backlash
items.summary: Meta removed a new AI image feature from its Muse Image generator days after global backlash, which allowed users to generate images from public Instagram profiles without notifying account holders.

items.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic's Latest Claude Versions
items.summary: The Trump administration ended a weeks-long ban on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions, lifting restrictions tied to cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: Indian AI Coding Startup Emergent Becomes Unicorn with $130M Series C
items.summary: Emergent, an Indian AI coding startup, secured $130 million in Series C funding, officially becoming a unicorn and highlighting the growth of India's AI infrastructure sector.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Shatters Launch Records with 2M Copies Sold in 24 Hours
items.summary: Ubisoft's remastered naval action game sold over two million copies in its first day on the market, surpassing the launch of Assassin's Creed Shadows and earning $1 million from DLC.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Goes Gold Ahead of September 3 Launch
items.summary: The open-world vampire RPG developed by The Witcher 3 veterans has officially entered its final development phase, confirming its release date for Xbox, PS5, and PC.

items.title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Reveals New 'Kill Block' Arena Feature
items.summary: Activision unveiled a new multiplayer mode where the battleground dynamically reconfigures before every match, with the game launching October 23 at the West Bridge Advanced Military Training Facility.

items.title: Sony Announces July PlayStation Plus Additions Including Avatar and Dying Light
items.summary: Rise of the Ronin is available today for Extra and Premium subscribers, while Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora joins on July 21, alongside seven other titles including Dying Light.

items.title: Avowed 2 Cancelled as Obsidian Shifts Focus to New Fallout RPG
items.summary: Obsidian Entertainment has reportedly cancelled the sequel to their fantasy RPG Avowed to develop a new post-apocalyptic game in the Fallout franchise.

items.title: Nvidia RTX Spark PCs to Include Sega Classics Like Virtua Fighter
items.summary: Nvidia announced that its new RTX Spark PC line will feature a curated selection of Sega games, including the iconic fighting game Virtua Fighter.

items.title: Apple Arcade Surprises Users with Madden NFL 27 Arcade Edition
items.summary: Madden NFL 27 Arcade Edition will launch on Apple Arcade on August 6, arriving as the World Cup nears its conclusion.

items.title: Fortnite Teases Christopher Nolan's 'Odissea' Crossover Ahead of July 17 Premiere
items.summary: A teaser for a Fortnite crossover with Christopher Nolan's upcoming film 'Odissea' has been released, coinciding with the movie's premiere date.

items.title: Dragon's Dogma 2 Exceeds Expectations on Switch 2 with 30 FPS Minimum
items.summary: The producer confirmed that Dragon's Dogma 2 is performing better than anticipated on the new Nintendo hardware, maintaining a minimum of 30 frames per second.

items.title: GTA 6 Now Open for Pre-Order Despite Unconfirmed 2025 Release Date
items.summary: Grand Theft Auto VI is available for pre-order across major platforms, though Rockstar Games has not officially confirmed the specific launch date, with 2025 being the widely speculated window.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Signs $30B Chip Pact with Broadcom and Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft
items.summary: Apple signed a landmark $30 billion multi-year agreement with Broadcom to boost domestic semiconductor production and sued OpenAI alleging it orchestrated a campaign to steal trade secrets about unreleased products via former employees.

items.title: Microsoft Cuts 4,800 Jobs Globally, Including 1,600 in Xbox Gaming Division
items.summary: Microsoft is cutting 4,800 jobs worldwide as part of a broader restructuring effort, with 1,600 of those cuts specifically affecting its Xbox gaming division.

items.title: Stripe and Advent International Make Joint $53 Billion Offer to Acquire PayPal
items.summary: Payments company Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have submitted a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc. at $60.50 per share, valuing the company at over $53 billion.

items.title: SpaceX Shares Drop Below IPO Price for First Time After Record Listing
items.summary: SpaceX shares fell below their initial public offering price for the first time on Wednesday, just over a month after the company completed the biggest IPO ever.

items.title: Meta Withdraws Controversial Instagram-Linked Muse AI Image Feature
items.summary: Meta has pulled a controversial feature from its newly launched Muse AI image generator that allowed users to create images by referencing public Instagram accounts without consent, following widespread backlash over privacy.

items.title: ASML Raises 2026 Financial Forecasts and Plans Capacity Expansion
items.summary: ASML, the world's largest chip equipment supplier, raised its 2026 financial forecasts and announced capacity expansion after reporting better-than-expected Q2 earnings driven by strong demand.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Approved for Use in China via Alibaba Partnership
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator approved Apple's on-device generative AI service "Apple Intelligence" for iPhones in the country, following a partnership with Alibaba to bring Qwen AI models to Apple's operating systems.
